key_insight: A physics-informed multihead neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent geometry of PDE solution families, where only a few principal components capture most of the variability beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reproducible way to compress and analyze solution manifolds for PDE surrogates, reduced-order modeling, and uncertainty-aware simulation across different initial conditions.
method: Shared-body multihead PINN learns a latent manifold for a family of PDE solutions.
Linear heads reconstruct individual solutions while the initial condition is embedded by construction.
A head-orthogonalization penalty reduces degeneracy and stabilizes the latent spectrum; Fourier-shell analysis maps frequency content to principal components.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, a latent dimension of 20 yielded strong compression: about 2–4 principal components explained roughly 95% of variance and 4–7 explained about 99%, with similar behavior for heat and wave equations.
watch_item: The latent basis is not unique even after orthogonalization, so interpretation should focus on basis-invariant spectral profiles and the method may be sensitive to training setup or head-orthogonalization strength.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
